schema_notes: version 2 adds 'tier' on every benchmark entry (#2566). Tier values: required | optional | xfail | blocker — same four-bucket model as validation/profiles/performance.toml. Version 1 readers tolerate the extra field; v2-aware tools (perf_floor_check, perf_suite_geomean_check, baseline_validator) gate on it.
schema_notes_v3: version 3 adds the memory dimension as a first-class perf axis, mirroring tests/harness/cpython perf gates and benches/3p/cross_runtime.rs peak-RSS sampling (FLOOR=1.0). Three OPTIONAL per-entry fields seam in here: 'mem_peak_rss_ratio' (float = cpython_rss / mamba_rss; >=1.0 means mamba uses <= CPython peak RSS), 'mem_status' (string: PASS | MEM_REGRESSION | CARVE_OUT | UNAVAIL), and 'mem_carve_out_reason' (string; cite the tracked subset-B regression when mem_status==CARVE_OUT — e.g. colorsys rgb_to_hsv, urllib.parse urlparse-bulk, mimetypes, measured 0.09-0.17x). Entries need NOT populate these fields; absence means memory was not sampled for that bench. v2 readers tolerate the extra fields; v3-aware tools gate MEM_REGRESSION only when not carved out.

benchmarks.name: colorsys_rgb_to_hsv
benchmarks.mem_carve_out_reason: subset-B tuple-return regression (project_mamba_colorsys_rgb_to_hsv_regression); matches tests/harness/cpython perf carve-out seed. Seam exemplar for the v3 memory fields — not a perf gate.
